entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The crop studied for seedborne infection by Rhizoctonia bataticola

entities.name: RHIZOCTONIA BATATICOLA
entities.description: The fungal pathogen causing seedborne infection, damping-off, and seed rot in groundnut

entities.name: SEEDBORNE INFECTION
entities.description: Infection of groundnut seeds caused by Rhizoctonia bataticola

entities.name: COMPONENT-PLATING METHOD
entities.description: A method used to determine the location of the pathogen on the seed coat, endosperm, and embryo

entities.name: PRE-EMERGENCE DAMPING-OFF
entities.description: A disease symptom occurring before seedling emergence, often manifesting as seed rot

entities.name: SEED ROT
entities.description: A specific form of pre-emergence damping-off

entities.name: POST-EMERGENCE DAMPING-OFF
entities.description: A disease symptom occurring after seedling emergence

entities.name: LATENT INFECTION
entities.description: Infection where the pathogen is present without apparent symptoms on the plant

entities.name: POTATO CARROT AGAR MEDIUM
entities.description: A medium used to prove the presence of latent infection from healthy seedlings

entities.name: SEED TRANSMISSION
entities.description: The process by which the pathogen is passed from one generation to the next via seeds

entities.name: CARBENDAZIM + TIN RAM
entities.description: A chemical treatment used to eliminate Rhizoctonia bataticola from groundnut seeds
